channel.title: Last Page
channel.description: Essays, blogs, reviews, notes and verse by O IV.

item.title: We Outsourced Our Minds to a New "Vitti Petti"
item.description: The television was once the idiot box. Today the smartest screen in our lives remembers, calculates, and thinks for us, leaving us to wonder how much of ourselves we have quietly handed over.

item.title: Oppenheimer
item.description: Christopher Nolan turns the birth of the atomic bomb into a three-hour trial of conscience, and somehow keeps you leaning forward the entire time.

item.title: The First Stone
item.description: On Jesus, Judgment, and Why a Single Sentence Still Feels More Radical Than Most Moral Certainties

item.title: The Last Dance
item.description: Messi and Ronaldo may be taking their final walk onto football's biggest stage, and the sport still owes us one impossible match

item.title: Nothing Ends in War and Peace
item.description: Tolstoy wrote a novel about war, history, love, death, and redemption, only to arrive at the unsettling possibility that life never really resolves at all

item.title: The Strongest Uchiha Was Never Madara
item.description: If Obito Uchiha had kept both his eyes, Naruto might have accidentally created the most broken character in the entire series

item.title: The Problem Is Not Freebies
item.description: We call welfare a freebie and corporate subsidies an investment, then wonder why nobody can have an honest conversation about economics

item.title: The Day I Realized I Hate Arsenal
item.description: Just a club whose defeats seem to bring me an unreasonable amount of joy

item.title: The Boys Was Never About Superheroes
item.description: A brilliant satire of power, celebrity, and corruption that stumbled at the finish line but never lost sight of what made it special

item.title: What If Walter Pater Was Right?
item.description: Art does not owe us morality, but society has a habit of stealing morality from art anyway

item.title: artsake
item.description: If art is supposed to influence culture, as the Romantics believed, then art is superior and should influence morals. However, I think art has no such commitments; it is as plain as Walter Pater's…

item.title: Running Man
item.description: His alarm rings at five while the streetlights are still buzzing, breaking his back all day just to hand the check to the bank, borrowing from Peter today so Paul stays off the porch tomorrow. A…

item.title: They Come at Seven in the Morning
item.description: On Pinarayi, Congress's Silence, and a Law That Was Never Really About Justice

item.title: The Man Who Rewrote the Game
item.description: On Pep Guardiola, the era he built, and the silence he leaves behind.

item.title: Long Live the King
item.description: Salah, the wait, and what it means to finally have a star who stayed.

item.title: The Thin Shell Between Order and Chaos
item.description: On the cycle humanity cannot escape and the shell that keeps breaking.

item.title: Pep
item.description: I never respected City. I respected Guardiola completely. The man who made Messi a God, built the greatest rivalry modern football has seen, and rewrote the game while everyone else was still trying…

item.title: Did Georgekutty needed a third Act?
item.description: Drishyam 3 is not a bad film. It just didn't need to exist.

item.title: Mo
item.description: Mo Salah did not just score goals for Liverpool. He made people fall in love with the club all over again. That is rarer than any record he broke. Long live the King.

item.title: CJP_2
item.description: Banning a protest, banning an idea, banning a revolution is the beginning of the fall of every empire ever built. History has never been kind to those who confused silence with consent. You can ban…

item.title: CJP_1
item.description: The Cockroach Janta Party, if nothing else, is a revolt and a frustration of Indian youth, and a burning need to build a third front. I am skeptical in my own ways about trusting an organisation…

item.title: The Nation That Demands Nothing of Its Leaders
item.description: On education, dynasty, and the democracy we keep choosing.

item.title: We Have Gandhi. Next Question.
item.description: What the Norway press briefing says about how India handles accountability.

item.title: The Man Who Came After the Gods Left
item.description: Lewandowski didn't get the throne. He got something quieter, and maybe more honest.

item.title: Does George R.R. Martin owe us the last two books?
item.description: On the Unspoken Contract Between a Writer and the Millions Who Kept His World Alive

item.title: Umar_Khalid_1
item.description: The Supreme Court has clarified that prolonged incarceration is a definitive ground for bail, which is highly relevant to Umar Khalid's case. If an accusation cannot be proven or brought to trial…

item.title: Umar_Khalid_2
item.description: The extremely strict bail rules under laws like UAPA and PMLA have introduced an unacceptable system of indefinite jail time for people who haven't even been proven guilty. This directly breaks the…

item.title: The System Is Rotten, But What Can Mere Parasites Do?
item.description: On the Indian Judiciary, the People It Protects, and the People It Calls Cockroaches

item.title: Subjugation is Heredity
item.description: The Leash We Call Loyalty: On How Obedience Was Never Forced Upon Us, Only Fed to Us Slowly, and the Quiet Architecture Built to Keep It That Way

item.title: When Oppression Learns to Call Itself Love
item.description: What Malayalam cinema sees that most cinema looks away from.

item.title: On Waiting for a Tomorrow That Keeps Its Distance
item.description: On Why Hope Keeps You in the Prison Longer Than Despair Ever Could

item.title: The Brothers Karamazov
item.description: Dostoevsky's last and largest novel is still the most complete argument for and against God ever written in fiction.

item.title: VijayCriticism
item.description: The people questioning Vijay's qualifications are the same people who have never once asked for the mark sheets of their own MLAs, MPs or even PMs. Selective scrutiny is not concern. It is bias…

item.title: The Dominoes Were Already Falling with the GPU Economy
item.description: A Speculation on Crypto, AI, and the Suspicion That Nothing Was Accidental

item.title: The Greatest Heist in History: How the weak seized the Thorne
item.description: Has Any Conqueror in History Done More Damage Than the One Who Never Raised a Weapon?

item.title: Attack on Titan
item.description: A story that starts as a monster show and ends as one of the bleakest arguments about cycles of violence ever put on screen.

item.title: Surviving Today
item.description: My biggest problem is surviving today. I have been here longer than I knew you, longer than most things I thought would last, and still I wake with the weight already there. Not grief. Something…
